facts_disclosed: Ran the Dubai Marathon on January 10, 2025 — finished in 4:12
Runs 5x a week as part of marathon training

turns.content: Hey! How are you doing? I haven't heard from you in a bit.
Hi there! I'm doing well, thanks for checking in. How have you been? What's going on?

I'm great! Actually I'm still kind of buzzing honestly. I ran the Dubai Marathon ten days ago and I'm still on a high from it haha. Like my legs are finally recovering but my brain is still at the finish line!!
That's amazing, congratulations! The Dubai Marathon on January 10th, right? That's a huge accomplishment. How did it go? Did you hit the time you were aiming for?

Yes January 10th!! Ai meu Deus, it was incredible. I finished in 4:12 which okay is not elite haha but for me it was everything. I was training SO hard for months — running five times a week, early mornings before work, long runs on Fridays. My whole life was basically eat, treat patients, run, sleep, repeat.

4:12 is a great time — that's nothing to downplay! Running five times a week while working full time takes serious discipline. How are you feeling physically now, ten days out? Are you back to running yet or still in recovery mode?

Oh gente, the first four days after I could barely walk down stairs haha!! I mean I'm a physical therapist so I KNOW about recovery protocols but knowing and doing are different things right? I did ice baths, compression, kept moving gently. Now I'm feeling much better, I did a light 5K yesterday and it felt amazing. My calves were a little tight but nothing concerning.

Sounds like you practiced what you preach with the recovery — ice baths and compression are solid. A light 5K at day nine is a good sign that you're bouncing back well. Are you thinking about what's next? Another race, or are you giving yourself a break from structured training for a while?

That's actually what I wanted to talk about! Because like, I've been running five times a week for so long now that I don't know what to DO with myself if I'm not training for something. I need a goal or I just wander around the gym like a lost puppy haha. I was thinking maybe a half marathon in March? Or do I do something completely different, like focus on strength for a few months?

That's a great problem to have — you've clearly built a strong running habit. A few things to consider:

1. **Recovery window** — Even though you feel good, your body has been under marathon-level stress for months. Most coaches recommend 2-4 weeks of reduced volume before jumping into another training cycle. A March half would mean starting structured training again very soon.

2. **Strength block** — Taking 6-8 weeks to focus on strength could actually make you a faster runner when you come back to race training. Stronger glutes, more resilient tendons, better running economy.

3. **Middle ground** — Keep running 3-4 times a week at easy pace (no speed work) and add 2 strength sessions. Then pick a race for April or May when your body has had more time to rebuild.

What feels more exciting to you right now?

Nossa, you sound like me talking to my patients haha! Okay the middle ground option is smart. I know if I sign up for March I'll go straight back into hard training because that's just who I am — I don't do things halfway. And honestly my IT band was giving me some trouble in the last few weeks of training, nothing serious but I could feel it. As a PT I would tell my patient to take the strength block. So I should listen to my own advice right??

Exactly — you already know the answer! An IT band that was "talking to you" during marathon training is your body's way of saying it needs some attention before you pile more running volume on top. A strength block targeting hip stability and glute activation would directly address that, and you'd come back to your next training cycle with a stronger foundation.

Maybe target a half marathon in late April or May? That gives you a solid 6-8 week strength phase, then a proper training buildup. You'll still be running during the strength block — just not at five-times-a-week marathon intensity.

You're so right. Okay I'm convinced — strength block for February and March, keep running 3x a week easy, and then I'll look at races for April or May. Maybe the Ras Al Khaimah half? I've heard that course is beautiful. And honestly this weather right now in Dubai is PERFECT for running, it would be a crime not to enjoy it before the heat comes back haha.

The RAK Half Marathon is a great option — it's a fast, scenic course and the weather in the northern emirates is usually ideal that time of year. Perfect target.

And yes, enjoy this running weather while it lasts! Use these cooler months for those easy runs and save the hard training push for when you've built that strength base. You've got a solid plan: strength focus now, race goal later, and a marathon finish to feel great about in the meantime. 4:12 is legit — don't let anyone tell you otherwise.

Haha obrigada querida!! I needed someone to talk me out of signing up for another race immediately. I get so excited I forget that rest is part of the training too. Okay strength block starts tomorrow. My legs will thank me later!

Your legs will definitely thank you — and so will that IT band. Enjoy the strength work, keep those easy runs fun, and you'll be in great shape when it's time to start the RAK training cycle. Congratulations again on the marathon!
